name: Taxonomy Mapping review for Mitophagy Enhancers coverage repair bundle
summary: The staged records are appropriate for the Mitophagy Enhancers track because the included urolithin A and USP30 evidence is tied to mitochondrial quality-control, mitophagy, mitochondrial biomarkers, or related muscle and immune endpoints. The bundle also keeps adjacent microbiome-metabolite, selective-autophagy, cognition, combination, and method-only items out of this repair pass unless they directly support the mitophagy outlook boundary.

findings.claim_or_issue: Urolithin A overlaps with microbiome-metabolite and selective-autophagy topics, but the staged records are used here only where they inform mitophagy or mitochondrial-quality-control evidence boundaries.
findings.why_it_matters: Shared interventions can inflate multiple tracks if the endpoint basis is not explicit.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep the support map tied to mitochondrial, mitophagy, muscle, immune, registry, and safety endpoints.

findings.claim_or_issue: Adjacent combination, restricted-eating, cognition, and method-focused records were excluded or deferred unless they directly repaired the Mitophagy Enhancers public support map.
findings.why_it_matters: Coverage repair should close the specific source-completeness gap without broadening the track beyond its taxonomy.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; revisit adjacent records only through their own track-specific surveillance or coverage repair.
